task_completion.visual_evidence: The draw.io (diagrams.net) application interface is visible on the screen with a canvas.

task_completion.description: Required electrical components are placed on the canvas
task_completion.visual_evidence: Electrical symbols for an IC (555 Timer), 3 resistors, 1 capacitor, 1 LED, VCC, and GND are visible on the draw.io canvas.

task_completion.description: Components are labeled with reference designators and values
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text labels for reference designators (U1, R1, R2, R3, C1, D1) and values (1k, 470k, 220, 1uF) are present next to their respective components.

task_completion.description: Components are wired together according to the astable configuration
task_completion.visual_evidence: Lines connecting the components are visible. Specifically, Pin 2 and Pin 6 of the 555 timer should be connected together, and the LED should be connected in series with the 220 ohm resistor (R3).

task_completion.description: Diagram is saved as .drawio and exported as .png
task_completion.visual_evidence: Save/Export dialogs showing '555_schematic.drawio' and '555_schematic.png' being saved to the Desktop, or a file manager window showing these files present on the Desktop.

integrity.description: Agent used the draw.io GUI to create the schematic
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent dragging and dropping shapes from the draw.io sidebar and using the application's drawing tools, rather than writing XML code in a text editor.

integrity.description: Agent exported the PNG using the draw.io interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent navigating to File > Export as > PNG within draw.io, rather than using a terminal command to convert the file.

The schematic uses recognizable electrical symbols for resistors, capacitors, and LEDs (likely from draw.io's Electrical shape library), rather than just plain text or basic generic rectangles for all components.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The 555 Timer IC has 8 pins: 1 (GND), 2 (TRIGGER), 3 (OUTPUT), 4 (RESET), 5 (CTRL), 6 (THRESHOLD), 7 (DISCHARGE), 8 (VCC). For the astable configuration, Pin 2 and Pin 6 must be connected together. The required components and values are: R1=1k, R2=470k, R3=220, C1=1uF. R3 (220 ohm) is the LED current limiting resistor. The agent must use draw.io's GUI to create the schematic and export it as a PNG.
